name: Don Ellis
abstract: Donald Johnson Ellis (July 25, 1934 – December 17, 1978) was an American jazz trumpeter, drummer, composer, and bandleader. He is best known for his extensive musical experimentation, particularly in the area of time signatures.

concerts.label: Jack Humphrey Septet/ Maynard Ferguson and His Orchestra/ Sarah Vaughan/ Modern Jazz Quartet
Midnight Performance: Jack Humphrey Septet/ Maynard Ferguson and His Orchestra/ Sarah Vaughan/ Modern Jazz Quartet
20th Century Innovations: Recent Developments in Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: Kiger decided to move back to Indiana, and he was replaced by Don Ellis. ... So then it was now Don Ellis, Eric Dolphy, and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lanny Morgan
about_quotes.evidence: Along with myself it was Eddie Harris and Leo Wright and Don Ellis and Cedar Walton. So it was really playing with good musicians
about_quotes.doc_title: Lanny Morgan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: But we had a quartet in the city and I was playing there... I was playing with Don Ellis, the trumpet player
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Karl Berger
about_quotes.evidence: And in those bands played a few jazz musicians who later became pretty well known, such as Cedar Walton or Lex Humphries and Don Ellis... Because we literally played with some of the best musicians in the world
about_quotes.doc_title: Karl Berger interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, August 11, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Miller and Pete Fountain
about_quotes.evidence: Don Ellis is mentioned by the interviewer as an example of a big band; the interviewee does not mention any personal interaction or professional relationship with Don Ellis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Miller and Pete Fountain Interview, 1970-01-09

about_quotes.speaker: Tim Kelly
about_quotes.evidence: The first band they had, uh, consisted of this Burt Sigerson... Don Ellis, who was an extremely good saxophonist... and the leader was a man named Bob Canfield
